content: Antibiotic resistance is spreading because bacteria are adapting to the medicines designed to kill them. When antibiotics are used too often or not taken correctly, some bacteria survive and develop the ability to resist these drugs. These resistant bacteria then multiply and spread, making infections harder to treat. Factors like overprescribing antibiotics, using them in farming to promote animal growth, and patients not completing their full course of treatment all contribute to this problem. Additionally, bacteria can share resistance traits with each other, accelerating the spread. This growing resistance means common infections may no longer respond to standard treatments, leading to longer illnesses, higher medical costs, and increased risk of death. Addressing this issue requires careful use of antibiotics, better hygiene practices, and investment in new medicines to stay ahead of evolving bacteria.
